title: Insufficient High-Quality Training Exemplars
assumption: High-quality datasheet exemplars exist in sufficient quantity (N >= 500) for effective LLM fine-tuning
description: Unable to find N>=500 high-quality datasheet exemplars for LLM fine-tuning, leading to poor suggestion quality
consequence: Copilot provides low-quality suggestions, leading to low acceptance rate (<40%) and mechanism failure at Step 1

mitigation.prevention: Pre-screen HuggingFace dataset cards using quality filters (completeness score, expert curation)
mitigation.detection: Monitor suggestion acceptance rate during pilot (<40% = red flag)
mitigation.response: PIVOT to hybrid approach (expert-authored seed templates + AI enhancement) or increase corpus via manual curation

early_warning: Pilot acceptance rate <50% in first 2 weeks

title: Inter-Rater Reliability Below Threshold
assumption: Documentation quality can be measured objectively with expert rubrics achieving inter-rater reliability kappa >= 0.7
description: Expert rubrics fail to achieve inter-rater reliability kappa >=0.7, making quality assessment unreliable
consequence: P2 quality rating predictions become untestable due to measurement unreliability

mitigation.prevention: Conduct pilot rating session with 3 raters on 20 samples, refine rubric until kappa >=0.7
mitigation.detection: Calculate Cohen's kappa after each rating batch
mitigation.response: PIVOT to structured rubrics with clearer definitions, add rater training, or use larger rater pool (N=5)

early_warning: High variance in ratings for same samples

title: Low Copilot Adoption Rate
assumption: Researchers will adopt copilot-assisted workflows when made default option
description: Researchers opt-out of copilot assistance or ignore suggestions, undermining practical impact
consequence: Low adoption rates undermine practical impact and scalability of the system

mitigation.prevention: Deploy as default-on with opt-out (not opt-in), provide onboarding tutorial
mitigation.detection: Track usage metrics (% of users engaging with copilot)
mitigation.response: EXPLORE UX improvements, gather user feedback, or PIVOT to lighter-weight assistance (smart templates)

early_warning: Low engagement metrics (<50% users clicking suggestions)

title: Repository Administrators Do Not Implement Validation
assumption: Repository administrators will implement Tier 1 syntactic validation as publish-blocking requirement
description: HuggingFace/OpenML decline to implement Tier 1 syntactic validation, keeping system opt-in
consequence: System remains opt-in pilot with limited scale; enforcement mechanism not tested

mitigation.prevention: Secure pilot agreements with repository administrators upfront, demonstrate value proposition
mitigation.detection: Track administrator engagement during pilot planning
mitigation.response: SCOPE reduction to willing repositories only, or PIVOT to post-publication validation layer

early_warning: Administrators express reluctance in pilot discussions

title: AI Assistance Enables Low-Quality Gaming
assumption: Making documentation easy increases compliance more than AI assistance decreases quality (net positive effect)
description: Researchers generate validator-compliant but semantically meaningless documentation, defeating quality goals
consequence: Adversarial testing reveals gaming behavior; researchers generate low-quality but validator-compliant documentation

mitigation.prevention: Two-tier validation (syntactic + semantic post-review), adversarial testing in pilot
mitigation.response: PIVOT to stricter semantic validation, add human-in-loop review, or ABORT if gaming >40%

early_warning: Semantic review flags >30% submissions as low-quality
